chunks.text: HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Cr. Jail Appeal No.S-50 of 2020. DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For hearing of M.A No.1524/25. 2. For hearing of M.A No. 1525/25. 3. For hearing of M.A No.1526/25. 28.03.2025. Mr. Imtiaz Ali Channa, Advocate for appellants. Mr. Mir Murtaza Abro, Advocate for complainant. Mr. Siraj Ahmed Bijarani, A.P.G for the State. J U D G M E N T Miran Muhammad Shah, J:- Compromise application has been filed U/s 345 Cr.P.C duly signed/thumb impressions made by the appellants/accused as well as the LRs of the deceased and also injured. So also an application U/s 345 has also been filed for the acquittal of the appellants/accused alongwith their affidavits. The LRs due to intervention of the Nek Mards have forgiven all the accused in the name of Almighty ALLAH. The report was called from the learned trial court/Additional Sessions Judge-I, Dadu. The report states that the widow of the deceased being a Parda Nasheen lady was unable to attend this court, therefore, the commissioner was appointed to obtain her thumb impression on her affidavit which was done so through the prescribed procedure. The other LRs, however, appeared before this court and submitted their affidavits. The two minors/LRs namely Mohsin aged about 12 years & Sitara aged about 14 years although happened to be minors, whose share needs to be protected as stated by the learned A.P.G present. For the said reason, learned Additional Registrar of this Court is directed to calculate the due share of the minors and submit report. 2. Be that as it may, the compromise applications filed are allowed and the appellants/accused may be released forthwith if not required in any other custody case.

For the said reason, learned Additional Registrar of this Court is directed to calculate the due share of the minors and submit report. 2. Be that as it may, the compromise applications filed are allowed and the appellants/accused may be released forthwith if not required in any other custody case. This acquittal shall be subject to submitting of the PR bond in the sum of Rs.100,000/- (Rupees One Lac only) each to the satisfaction of the learned Additional Registrar of this Court. Learned counsel for appellants/accused also requests that the amount as calculated by learned Additional Registrar may be allowed to be deposited in four installments. The request of learned Counsel for appellants/accused is allowed, however, the first installment be deposited within a period of seven days, while remaining three installments would be paid with the gap of three months each. JUDGE Ali.
